response: Alex has always had a strong belief in traditional values, especially when it comes to love and relationships. This month, they decided to challenge their views by reading three different novels. Each novel presented a unique perspective on how love and relationships can be understood. The first novel was a short but impactful read, containing 120 pages. It took Alex four days to finish this book. Despite its length, the novel was quite engaging. The second novel was longer, with 180 pages. It took Alex six days to complete this one. The third novel was the longest of the three, with 240 pages. It took Alex eight days to read through. All three novels were read at a consistent pace. Alex read the same number of pages each day for each novel. This consistency made it easier to track the progress. The first novel was completed in four days, which means Alex read 30 pages per day. The second novel was read over six days, resulting in 30 pages per day. The third novel was completed in eight days, which also means 30 pages per day. The daily reading rate was the same for all three novels. This pattern shows that Alex maintained a steady pace throughout. The total number of pages read across all three novels was 660. To find the average pages per day, the total pages must be divided by the total number of days. The total number of days spent reading was 18. Dividing 660 by 18 gives the average. The average number of pages read per day is 36.67. This number represents the average across all three novels. It is important to note that the daily reading rate was consistent. Each novel was read at the same pace. This consistency is what allowed for an accurate average. The first novel had 120 pages and was read over four days. The second novel had 180 pages and was read over six days. The third novel had 240 pages and was read over eight days. The total number of pages read was 120 plus 180 plus 240. That equals 660 pages.
